steps.title: Track the structure
steps.content: The text begins with a common criticism: franchise movies are often called predictable. It then pushes back, arguing that familiar elements do not make a movie unworthy and that some franchise movies are original.

steps.title: Reject narrower structures
steps.content: Choice A says the text summarizes and continues a plot, but no plot is summarized. Choice B frames the passage around people enjoying or disliking one movie. Choice C invents an audience-reaction section. The text presents a criticism and then argues that it is mostly overstated. The answer is D.
